intro: The biggest real split between Azure Cosmos DB and Xata is lock-in posture: Azure Cosmos DB carries low lock-in (multi-model but azure-bound), while Xata carries medium lock-in (postgres under hood, exportable). That single fact shapes the rest of the comparison. Azure Cosmos DB is built for apps, global and is not a fit for simple, offline projects. Xata is built for apps, global and is not a fit for simple, offline projects. On the scoring side, Azure Cosmos DB rates 4/5 for developer experience and 4/5 for transparency; Xata rates 5/5 and 4/5 respectively. The honest trade-off: with Azure Cosmos DB you accept that simple key-value storage needs, while Xata comes with the reality that simple key-value storage needs. Neither side is free. Azure Cosmos DB's headline strength in the data is that apps needing managed database. Xata's headline strength is that apps needing managed database. Read each side's best-for and not-for fields carefully before you pick; the shape of your team and workflow matters more than any single score.
quick_take: Azure Cosmos DB is for apps, global; Xata is for apps, global; decide based on lock-in tolerance.
verdict_a: Choose Azure Cosmos DB if you are apps, global and can accept low lock-in (multi-model but azure-bound).
verdict_b: Choose Xata if you are apps, global and need medium lock-in (postgres under hood, exportable).

faq.q: Is Azure Cosmos DB cheaper than Xata?
faq.a: Azure Cosmos DB uses a subscription model, while Xata uses a freemium model, and both publish a free tier. We don't have per-seat numbers in this dataset, so 'cheaper' depends on volume. Price both on your actual usage before deciding.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Azure Cosmos DB to Xata?
faq.a: Azure Cosmos DB has low lock-in (multi-model but azure-bound); Xata has medium lock-in (postgres under hood, exportable). Migration effort scales with how much state and automation you've built in Azure Cosmos DB; plan for data export, config rebuild, and a parallel-run period before cutting over.

In our data, Xata scores 5/5 for developer experience versus 4/5 for Azure Cosmos DB. That said, DX is subjective; the Azure Cosmos DB score may still be acceptable if its feature fit is stronger for your use case.

faq.q: Is Xata a good alternative to Azure Cosmos DB?
faq.a: Xata is built for apps, global and explicitly not for simple, offline projects, so it's a fit only if your workflow matches its stated audience. If you were using Azure Cosmos DB for apps, global, the switch is reasonable; outside that scope, look elsewhere.
